channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Delhi High Court orders daily health monitoring for activist Sonam Wangchuk during hunger strike
item.description: The Delhi High Court has directed the Centre and Delhi government to ensure daily health monitoring for activist Sonam Wangchuk, who is on a hunger strike since June 28 demanding Union Education Minis...

item.title: Algerian Orphanage Fire Results in 11 Child Deaths, 19 Injuries
item.description: A devastating fire at an orphanage in Algeria has resulted in the tragic deaths of 11 children and injuries to 19 others, according to recent reports. The incident, which occurred on July 16, 2026, ha...

item.title: Deputy PM Ishaq Dar signs Pakistan's founding membership in China-led AI body during Shanghai visit
item.description: Deputy Prime Minister and Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar has arrived in Shanghai to sign Pakistan's founding membership in the World Artificial Intelligence Cooperation Organisation (WAICO), a China-led i...

item.title: A Novel Narrated by a Book: Exploring Stories' Lasting Impact
item.description: Angela O’Keeffe’s *Phantom Days* is a literary exploration of storytelling’s power, where a book becomes an active participant in the narrative. The novel follows Isabel, who grapples with a phantom p...

item.title: Kenyans Raise Funds to Provide Oxygen Concentrator for Woman with Heart Condition
item.description: Ann Wangeci Wanjiku, a Kenyan woman with a hole in her heart, has received a rechargeable oxygen concentrator after Kenyans pooled resources to help her. The device, which replaces bulky oxygen cylind...

item.title: UK reaffirms sovereignty over Falkland Islands amid Argentina's territorial claims
item.description: The UK government has reiterated its stance that the Falkland Islands are British territory following Argentina's display of a banner supporting its territorial claim during a World Cup match. The ban...

item.title: Minister Umahi Orders Autopsy on Deceased Staff Member
item.description: Senator David Umahi, the Minister of Works, has officially asked Nigeria Police Force to conduct an autopsy on Miss Mary Habila, a staff member who died at his residence in Uburu, Ebonyi State. The re...

item.title: Immigrant Children's Education Disrupted by Anti-Immigrant Repatriation Crisis
item.description: The article highlights the disruption of education for children of immigrants in South Africa due to recent anti-immigrant actions. Over 50,000 immigrants have left the country, with many learners for...

item.title: Geita Gold Mine Secures $500M Financing, Boosting Tanzania's Mining Sector
item.description: Tanzania’s mining sector has received a major boost after AngloGold Ashanti’s Geita Gold Mine secured a $500 million financing facility arranged by Nedbank Corporate and Investment Banking (CIB). The ...

item.title: Artemisinin's Role in Combating Mastitis Through Anti-Inflammatory and Anti-Ferroptotic Mechanisms
item.description: This study reveals how artemisinin (ART) alleviates inflammation and ferroptosis in mastitis by activating the ZDHHC12/Nrf2/HO-1 axis. Researchers demonstrate that ART reduces iron accumulation, lipid...

item.title: Johannesburg and Marble Towers Agree to Joint Inspections to Address Illegal Structures
item.description: The City of Johannesburg and Marble Towers have reached a court agreement to pause demolitions at the iconic CBD building while both parties conduct joint inspections to identify alleged illegal struc...

item.title: GOP Lawmaker Criticizes Veterans' Benefits Bill Opposition
item.description: Rep. Dan Crenshaw (R-TX), a retired Navy SEAL, launched a scathing attack on the Veterans of Foreign Wars (VFW) after House Republicans pulled the Take Care of America's Veterans Act from the floor. T...

item.title: Childcare worker faces over 300 charges after alleged abuse spanning a decade
item.description: A former Sydney childcare worker, Hamish Tait, 35, faces over 300 child abuse charges spanning 16 years, including producing child abuse material, filming children without consent, and sexually touchi...

item.title: Linings on Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool Fade Amid Renovation Controversy
item.description: The Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool, once coated with a color President Donald Trump dubbed 'American flag blue,' has faded significantly since its renovation began this spring. The pool's liner, whi...

item.title: Sam Neill passes away at 78 after pneumonia, having overcome lymphoma
item.description: Sam Neill, the iconic actor known for his role as Dr. Alan Grant in 'Jurassic Park,' passed away at the age of 78 due to pneumonia. His agent, Philip Grenz, confirmed the cause of death and clarified ...

item.title: Progress and Challenges in Sara Duterte's Impeachment Trial
item.description: The impeachment trial of Vice President Sara Duterte, which entered its second week, is expected to be a prolonged process spanning over seven months. Prosecutors plan to present their final witness, ...

item.title: Ebonyi Police Begin Post-Mortem on Mary Habila After Death at Umahi's Residence
item.description: The Ebonyi State Police Command has confirmed they will conduct a post-mortem examination on Mary Habila, a 26-year-old physiotherapist who died at the residence of Minister of Works David Umahi. Desp...

item.title: Security Deployment in Ol Kalou as By-Election Concludes Amid Protests
item.description: Residents of Ol Kalou witnessed a heavy security presence as voting concluded in the constituency's parliamentary by-election. The deployment of water cannons, police trucks, and over 1,000 officers a...

item.title: Zanzibar President Urges Investment in Leadership and Health Systems to Honor Mkapa’s Legacy
item.description: Zanzibar President Hussein Ali Mwinyi emphasized the need for sustained investment in leadership development, robust healthcare systems, and youth empowerment programs to honor former President Benjam...

item.title: ‘Backrooms’ Blu-ray and 4K Editions Offer Nearly Three Hours of Special Features
item.description: The horror film ‘Backrooms’, directed by 20-year-old Kane Parsons, has achieved significant commercial success since its May release, grossing $331 million from a $10 million budget. A24 has confirmed...
